items.visible_value: Large collection of emotion-expression reaction GIFs commonly used in KakaoTalk, Discord, and social media.
project_use: Add Tenor to Gifer and Giphy as an active discovery source when story imagery is insufficient.
Search by emotion, facial action, body reaction, punchline role, and intensity.
Use loops for Reels or motion cards and representative frames for static carousel slides.

items.visible_value: Collection of free first-three-second hooking videos intended to create a viral-style opening.
project_use: Reference source for opening movement, handoff transitions, reveals, camera wipes, and visual interruption patterns.
Use only when the motion reinforces the actual topic and does not become a generic unrelated hook.
Store each useful hook by motion type, required crop, duration, energy, and suitable emotion.

items.design: soft multicolor gradient, one centered oversized black statement, generous empty space, no competing imagery
project_use: Reference for a visually quiet final slide after information-dense resource cards.
CTA should state one benefit and one action rather than stacking multiple requests.
The final slide may simplify radically while retaining the carousel's typography.

items.visible_offer: ordinary room photo to Instagram bonus explanation with a watch-now CTA
funnel_structure: overlay a compact explainer card and direct CTA
project_use: Potential future income-research topic for monetizing original visual posts in addition to affiliate content.
Reusable educational-ad structure for explaining a verified platform opportunity.
items.guardrail: The screenshot does not prove current Instagram bonus eligibility, region coverage, payout, or account access; verify before planning or claiming revenue.

design_learning: mix purple, pink, cream, black, and neon accents while repeating oversized white headline typography
alternate person-led videos, diagrams, carousel covers, and software screenshots to avoid one-note grids
use recognizable platform and tool icons as navigation cues
make each tile's promise understandable before opening the post
items.project_use: Competitor reference for packaging an end-to-end social-content system as understandable user outcomes rather than internal modules.
items.guardrail: Visible tool and approval claims are not evidence that equivalent capabilities are connected here.

grid_grammar: cinematic full-bleed image or video
small restrained account watermark
short white headline anchored to the lower-left
minimal decoration and no heavy title box
varied visual genres held together by one text placement system
project_use: Strong alternative news layout for technology and AI topics when the source image is visually compelling.
Shows that grid consistency can come from headline placement and type treatment without forcing one image style.
items.guardrail: Headlines visible in the grid remain source observations and were not fact-checked in this batch.

visible_structure: cover variants with transparent image placeholders
setup notes and usage instructions beside the template family
project_use: Reference for maintaining a reusable editable layout family instead of generating every layout from zero.
Reference for separating image placeholders, typography, content blocks, and onboarding notes.
A template can remain fixed structurally while image, copy length, color, and slide count vary.

grid_grammar: dark cinematic or monochrome imagery
white headline in the lower-left
lavender accent on one key phrase
small rounded account label
short supporting line below the headline
objects and solitary people used as metaphors for technical topics
image_prompt_learning: cinematic editorial still with one symbolic subject
negative space reserved in the lower-left for headline
subtle film grain and architectural depth
one accent color repeated in typography and small UI elements
items.project_use: Reference for premium AI and knowledge-account visuals that feel editorial rather than like generic software screenshots.

grid_grammar: large outlined Korean thumbnail text
white main text with yellow key phrase
one central mascot, person, comparison, or result image
tool icons quickly establish the workflow
high variation in color and subject while headline treatment remains stable
project_use: Reference for tutorials where the promised result must be understood at thumbnail size.
Mascots, before-and-after details, and tool-icon equations can explain otherwise abstract AI workflows.
items.quality_note: High attention value, but dense text and many icons should be reduced when mobile readability suffers.

grid_grammar: white textured paper background
orange or ochre highlight strip
bold black Korean editorial headline
clean 3D symbolic object or a clear before-and-after hero
minimal footer handle and strong three-column consistency
image_prompt_learning: isolated 3D symbolic object on subtly textured off-white paper
soft studio shadow directly beneath the object
playful but controlled clay or polished-plastic material
one visual metaphor representing the tutorial topic
content_learning: package prompt resources into numbered practical collections
use a visual example to explain the output before exposing the prompt
cover stickers, hooks, AI skills, LEGO style, image effects, learning resources, and workflow guides as searchable libraries
items.project_use: High-value reference for our reusable prompt library and for visually explaining abstract workflow or knowledge topics.

cross_batch_learning: Tenor joins Gifer and Giphy as an active emotion-media discovery source.
The first three seconds can use a transition pattern, but it must remain semantically connected to the story.
A final CTA slide benefits from visual quiet after dense informational slides.
Platform monetization claims are research leads until eligibility and payout are verified.
Template families should separate stable structure from variable media, copy, color, and slide count.
News, knowledge, and tutorial accounts need different image grammars even when all cover AI topics.
Prompt libraries become more useful when each prompt is paired with category fit, composition rules, and an example output.
